is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of P. falciparum infections; 'We only considered treatments for new P. falciparum infections' and species confirmed by PCR.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Table 1 reports dhfr 59R (108N,51I assumed present), dhps 437G, 540E, and pfmdr1 86N with prevalence counts. Target markers include Pfdhfr 51,59,108; Pfdhps 437,540; Pfmdr1 86.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives numerator/denominator: dhfr 59R 279/322, dhps 437G 310/323, 540E 304/323, pfmdr1 86N 96/322.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples come from a single neighborhood cohort in Kampala, Uganda; frequencies in Table 1 are pooled across the whole cohort. Although the study distinguishes distance from a swamp for treatment failure, marker frequencies are reported only as one pooled total, not tied to sub-national sub-units per locus.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: The pooled marker prevalences (Table 1) span Nov 2004–Mar 2007 (~29 months, within 3y for the pooled figure), but samples collected Nov 2004 to Mar 2007 — the pooled total is within 3 years (approx 2.3y). Figure 3 gives per-quarter/per-period polymorphism prevalence but not per-locus counts.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from children in Kampala, Uganda (African country).

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary longitudinal clinical trial cohort with genotyping.

duplicate_risk.evidence: 'as part of a longitudinal clinical trial'; 'an interim analysis of comparative results through June 2006 has been published [21]' (Dorsey et al. JAMA 2007) — samples are part of a larger randomized trial cohort with prior publications.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary tables referenced for the marker frequency data; Table 1 contains the extractable counts.

assessment.summary: Longitudinal AQ+SP treatment cohort of 129 children in a Kampala, Uganda neighborhood (Nov 2004–Mar 2007). Reports extractable per-locus resistance marker frequencies (dhfr 51/59/108, dhps 437/540, pfmdr1 86, pfcrt 76 assumed fixed). However, the marker frequencies are pooled across the single Kampala cohort with no sub-national breakdown per locus (fails sub-country resolution beyond the city itself), and the pooled marker prevalences are not binned to <=3y in Table 1 (though total span is ~2.3y). Samples are part of a larger published randomized trial (high duplicate risk).
exclusion_reasons: Marker frequencies reported only as pooled totals for a single Kampala neighborhood cohort; no per-locus sub-national breakdown
Per-locus marker frequencies (Table 1) are pooled across the entire study period rather than binned by finer temporal windows
High duplicate risk: samples part of larger previously published randomized trial cohort (Dorsey et al. JAMA 2007)
